answer:
\boxed{A}

Folding a standard sheet of paper (about 0.1 mm thick) in half 50 times would result in a thickness of **~10^15 meters**—far exceeding the Earth-Sun distance (~150 million km or ~1.5 × 10^11 meters). The claim is exaggerated, as practical limits (like paper strength) prevent more than ~10–12 folds. Thus, the statement is false.
